title: To His Book (4)
content:
Go thou forth, my book, though late,
    Yet be timely fortunate.
    It may chance good luck may send
    Thee a kinsman or a friend,
    That may harbour thee, when I
    With my fates neglected lie.
    If thou know'st not where to dwell,
    See, the fire's by.--Farewell!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To His Book (4)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
